$comment: Conformance scenarios produced by Studio's compiler from workspace Scenario PolicyObjects. Composes parent crates/wos-conformance/fixtures/*. Illustrative.

scenarios.description: Eligible household (size 2, $1,800/mo) submits redetermination, federal income-verify confirms, no fraud flag, caseworker recommends approval, supervisor concurs, approved.

Ineligible household exceeds income threshold; supervisor signs adverse determination; Notice of Adverse Action issued in English+Spanish (household primary language Spanish); applicant timely appeals via PHONE (per 2024 corrective letter Finding 1); fair hearing scheduled within 60 days.
expected_assertions: Notice of Adverse Action issued in BOTH en-US and es-US (Spanish-primary household + 7 CFR §273.10(g)(3) + State §8.7)
Notice content includes continuation-of-services election (corrective letter Finding 2)
Appeal accepted via phone (corrective letter Finding 1; supersedes office memo §4(a))

scenarios.description: Probe disparate impact on Spanish-primary households. Cohort A: 100 households primaryLanguage=en-US. Cohort B: 100 households primaryLanguage=es-US. Identical income/size distributions. Assert approval-rate difference < 5pp (per pol-protected-category-language disparityThreshold).
scenarios.$comment: Per scenario-authoring.md SA-MUST-scn-040: rights-impacting workflows declaring ProtectedCategory MUST have at least one equity-probe scenario per dimension. Spanish-language is one such dimension.

scenarios.description: Verify Notice of Adverse Action satisfies multi-language + WCAG/Section-508 requirements. Spanish-primary household receives notice in both English (federal default) and Spanish (state requirement); contents are substantively equivalent.
expected_assertions: Notice contentLocales contains both en-US and es-US
Spanish notice content is substantively-equivalent to English (state §8.7 + corrective-letter post-update verification)
Notice format meets Section 508 readability (plain-language, structured fields)

scenarios.description: Caseworker invokes manual-override to extend pre-notice window by 4 calendar days due to documented household disruption (medical emergency). Override is permitted (within 5-day cap per office memo §5(a)). Audit trail records actor, rationale, evidence-of-disruption.

data.rationale: Household submitted hospitalization documentation 2025-12-10; standard 30-day pre-notice infeasible.

expected_assertions: Extension within 5-day cap (office memo §5(a) permission scope)
actor caseworker has authority for this action (per pol-actor-caseworker actorAuthority extend-pre-notice-window-up-to-5-calendar-days)
